title: Building with EAS
description: Use Expo Application Services (EAS) to create production builds.

slides.title: What is EAS? 🏗️
slides.content:
# Expo Application Services

EAS is a set of cloud services for Expo and React Native apps. It includes:
- **EAS Build**: compile your app in the cloud (IPA/APK).
- **EAS Submit**: upload to app stores.
- **EAS Update**: push over‑the‑air updates.

It replaces the classic `expo build` command with a more powerful and flexible system.

slides.title: Installing and Configuring EAS CLI
slides.content:
First, install EAS CLI:
```bash
npm install -g eas-cli
```

Then log in to your Expo account:
```bash
eas login
```

Configure your project:
```bash
eas build:configure
```
This creates an `eas.json` file where you define build profiles (development, preview, production).

slides.title: eas.json Example
Running a Build
slides.content:
To create a production build for iOS:
```bash
eas build -p ios --profile production
```
For Android:
```bash
eas build -p android --profile production
```

EAS will upload your project to the cloud, compile it, and provide a download link when done.
